name: Infisical API
what_it_does: Infisical — open-source, self-hostable secrets management platform for storing, versioning, and syncing environment variables and secrets across teams and deployment environments.
use_cases: Agents fetching secrets dynamically at runtime instead of hardcoding credentials in configuration files
Secret rotation automation — agents rotating API keys and credentials via Infisical's rotation engine
Cross-environment secret sync — agents propagating secrets from development to staging and production consistently
Secret audit trails — agents querying Infisical audit logs to track who accessed which credentials and when
Dynamic secrets — agents requesting short-lived database credentials generated on-demand via Infisical's dynamic secrets engine
not_for: Teams already deeply integrated with HashiCorp Vault — migration cost outweighs Infisical's benefits
Non-secret configuration management — Infisical is for secrets, not general app config
Extremely high-throughput secret access (>10K fetches/s) — use Vault with local caching for high-scale
best_when: Your agent stack needs open-source, self-hosted secrets management with a developer-friendly API, secret versioning, and automated rotation — without HashiCorp Vault's operational complexity.
avoid_when: You're already on Vault or AWS Secrets Manager with working integrations — switching has migration costs.

auth.notes: Universal Auth (machine identity) for service-to-service. Service tokens (legacy). JWT tokens for user sessions. OIDC federation for cloud-native auth (AWS IAM, GCP, K8s). Scoped to project and environment.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: free forever; Cloud Free: 5 members, 3 projects, unlimited secrets
pricing.paid_tiers: Cloud Team: $8/seat/month; Enterprise: custom with SSO, SCIM, RBAC
pricing.notes: Open source under MIT-like license — self-hosted is completely free. Cloud adds managed hosting. Enterprise adds SAML SSO, SCIM, and advanced RBAC.

performance.rate_limits: Cloud: 300 req/min default; self-hosted: no limits beyond infrastructure

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API with OpenAPI spec. Error responses include specific validation messages. SDK error types are well-structured. Secret not found errors are clearly distinguished from auth errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Secret creation/update via setSecret is idempotent. Secret fetching is fully idempotent. Batch secret operations support atomic updates.
known_agent_gotchas: Universal Auth requires machine identity setup before use — not just an API key, needs identity configuration
Secrets are environment-scoped — agents must specify correct environment (dev/staging/prod) or get wrong secrets
Secret references (cross-secret interpolation) are resolved at fetch time — circular references cause errors
Dynamic secrets require a configured secret lease — agents must manage lease renewal for long-running processes
Self-hosted instances on older versions may lack newer API endpoints — check running version before using new features

security_score_components.security_notes: Open source — fully auditable. End-to-end encryption for secrets. OIDC federation for zero-secret auth. SOC2 Type II for cloud. Self-hosted for full data sovereignty. Dynamic secrets with automatic rotation.
